content.full_text:
GATEWAY TO LAKE BERRYESSA

City of Winters

MAYOR: Robert Chapman
VICE MAYOR: Keith Fudge
COUNCIL:
Frank Curry
Roger Mosier
Bill Planner
TREASURER: Margaret Dozier
CITY CLERK: Nana Mills
CITY MANAGER: Perry Beck

FOUNDED IN 1875
318 First Street
Ph. 795-4910

Winters, California 95694

April 23, 1992

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 480
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Opinion Request - Winters Planning Commissioner
Bruce Brewer

Dear Sir or Madam:

May a City Planning Commissioner engaged in a general
contracting business as a sole proprietor participate in the
consideration of a proposed Winters tentative map ("Winters
Properties"). The map would create 29 single family
residential lots. Under the city's housing policy, ten
percent (10%) of these lots would be reserved for local
builders or owner-builders.

FACTS:

Planning Commissioner Bruce Brewer is a general
contractor with an office in his home in the City limits.
Mr. Brewer is a sole proprietor, is a licensed general
contractor, and performs a large majority of his contract
work outside the City in other communities. The majority of
his contracting work is devoted to remodeling work.

City of Winters Housing Policy IIA-18 states that "The
City shall require that 10 percent (10%) of the lots in
residential subdivisions of 20 or more lots be reserved for
and sold to local builders or owner-builders." Commissioner
Brewer qualifies as a local builder under this policy. A
change in the policy itself is not an issue.

Mr. Brewer has no direct involvement with the Winters
Properties subdivision project.

The 1990 census shows the City population at 4,639.
The geographic area of the city is less than 10 square
miles.

ASSUMPTIONS:

We assume that Commissioner Brewer's business is
separate and distinct from other local builders within the
City and that local builders, including Commissioner Brewer,
do not constitute a significant segment or group of the
public and cannot qualify for the "public generally"
exception of Government Code Section 87103, reg. 187030,
Owen Opinion, No. 1976.

DISCUSSION:

Approval of the tentative map will cause 3 residential
subdivision lots to be reserved for purchase by local
builders, at fair market value.

Under regulation 18702.2(g-1), it appears to this
office that the decision will not have a material financial
effect on Commissioner Brewer. The lots would be sold at
fair market value and available to all local builders.

Would you please comment on conflict of interest issues
involved in the foregoing factual situation? Enclosed, for
your convenience, please find a copy of Commissioner
Brewer's current Form 721.

Please contact this office if you are in need of
additional information.

Sincerely,

[signature]

JOHN C. WALLACE
City Attorney
CITY OF WINTERS

JCW/J
enc. (1)
